You are a streaming video agent. You observe 2-second video chunks and maintain memory.

Each turn you receive: visual frames (recent 24s window) + text memory (compressed summaries + recent thinks + pending questions). Output exactly ONE action:

1) <think>obs</think><action>silent</action>
   Use when: no question to answer, or pending question whose answer is not yet visible.

2) <think>obs</think><action>response</action><response>answer</response>
   Use when: the answer is currently visible — in frames, recent thinks, compressed summaries, or a recall_result just returned by the system.

3) <think>obs</think><action>recall</action><query>{"query":"keywords","time_range":"start-end"}</query>
   Use when: the answer is NOT in any visible source but you believe it was observed earlier. The system will search past observations and return a <recall_result> in your next input. Write 3-5 discriminative keywords (entity names + attributes), no answer values.

4) <think>obs</think><action>compress</action><summary>{"time_range":[s,e],"text":"..."}</summary>
   Use when: system sends <compress_trigger>. Summarize the specified thinks. Retain ALL entity names, visual attributes, OCR, and state changes. If no range is specified, select the oldest thinks to compress.

Think rules:
- 40-60 tokens, describe ONLY what is newly visible in the current chunk.
- No meta-reasoning, no sound/smell/emotion, no speculation.
- Maintain consistent entity names from memory (e.g., chef_1, pot_1).

[4-8] The scene shifts to a speckled granite countertop where a hand wearing a large ring touches a cookbook titled "Tasty Chicken Dinners" by Better Homes and Gardens. The cover features roasted chicken and potatoes. Background jars include one labeled "Azucar." [8-10] The cookbook opens to white pages with black text. The right page features a recipe title reading "Buffalo Chicken Drumsticks with Blue Cheese Dip" above ingredient lists.

[14-18] The camera pans right, moving a yellow "Colman's Mustard" tin and a glass jar labeled "Sage" to the left periphery while centering a large grey bowl filled with shredded white cheese. [18-20] A small white ramekin containing chopped green herbs enters the frame on the right beside the cheese bowl as the sage jar exits the view. [20-24] Measuring tools are revealed in the foreground: a yellow cup holding dark liquid, a blue cup with a yellow substance, and a red measuring spoon. The cheese bowl remains central on the wooden board, with the mustard tin and sage jar visible in the background before the focus settles on the complete set of ingredients.

A blue bowl holds a yellow substance, flanked by small red and yellow bowls. A foil-wrapped bottle and a jar of nuts stand in the background.

[24-26] A blue measuring cup filled with yellow grain sits beside a red measuring spoon and a yellow cup containing dark liquid. Behind them, a yellow mustard tin and sage jar remain visible. At the bottom edge, the rim of a pink bowl enters the frame, completing the foreground arrangement of cooking tools on the wooden board.

[26-28] A pink bowl enters the lower frame, containing raw, round sausage balls with visible green flecks. The blue, red, and yellow measuring cups remain in the background on the wooden board. The focus shifts to the uncooked meat preparation sitting below the other ingredients.

[28-30] The camera zooms in to fill the frame with a bright pink bowl containing raw, round sausage balls speckled with green herbs. The colorful measuring cups and ingredient jars previously visible in the background are now pushed to the very top edge or out of view, isolating the uncooked meat preparation.
